Title: Mutations of genes in synthesis of the carotenoid precursors of ABA lead to pre-harvest sprouting and photo-oxidation in rice

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: core carotenoid desaturation steps upstream of ABA precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: desaturates phytoene in the carotenoid pathway toward downstream colored carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes seed dormancy and prevents pre-harvest sprouting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss of OsPDS disrupts carotenoid biosynthesis, greatly reduces ABA, and causes albino seedlings with pre-harvest sprouting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phs1 was caused by a Tos17 insertion in intron 4 of OsPDS. 2. OsPDS expression was undetectable in the mutant. 3. Carotenoids were almost undetectable and phytoene accumulated, consistent with blockage of the PDS step. 4. ABA content was reduced and seeds showed vivipary/pre-harvest sprouting. 5. Genomic complementation rescued the mutant phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: RT-PCR signal detected in wild type; absent in phs1 mutant
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field screening under high humidity favored pre-harvest sprouting; carotenoids analyzed in light-grown seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: albino lethal seedlings; severe pigment deficiency; abnormal plastid development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homozygous mutant seedlings were non-viable and lacked plastid/chloroplast structures.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: core carotenoid desaturation steps upstream of ABA precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: more desaturated carotenoid intermediates leading to lycopene
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: desaturates ζ-carotene in the carotenoid pathway toward lycopene formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes seed dormancy and prevents pre-harvest sprouting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss of OsZDS impairs carotenoid biosynthesis, lowers ABA, and causes albino pre-harvest sprouting mutants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant identification; map-based cloning; insertion/deletion mapping; RT-PCR loss of expression; HPLC profiling; genetic complementation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phs2-1 carried a Tos17 insertion in exon 3 and phs2-2 carried a 1.7-kb deletion in OsZDS. 2. OsZDS expression was undetectable in both alleles. 3. Carotenoids were nearly absent and small peaks of ζ-carotene/isomers accumulated. 4. ABA content was reduced and mutants displayed vivipary. 5. Complementation restored the wild-type phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: RT-PCR signal detected in wild type; absent in phs2 alleles
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field humidity favored PHS screening; carotenoids analyzed in light-grown seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: albino lethal seedlings; no normal chloroplast development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homozygous mutants were non-viable and showed no plastid- or chloroplast-like structures in mesophyll cells.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: lycopene isomerization step in core carotenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: isomerizes cis-carotenoid intermediates such as prolycopene to all-trans forms required for downstream carotenoid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes seed dormancy and prevents pre-harvest sprouting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: mutation of OsCRTISO reduces lutein and ABA, causes variegation/photobleaching, oxidative stress, and pre-harvest sprouting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant allele sequencing; RT-PCR/splicing analysis; carotenoid HPLC profiling; ABA measurement; chlorophyll fluorescence; ROS staining; TEM; complementation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phs3-1 carried a splice-site mutation in OsCRTISO, and additional phs3 alleles independently affected the same gene. 2. Light-grown mutants showed strongly reduced carotenoids, especially lutein, and dark-grown seedlings accumulated prolycopene. 3. ABA content was reduced, causing vivipary while ABA responsiveness remained normal. 4. Mutants showed abnormal chloroplasts, photobleaching, ROS accumulation, reduced NPQ and Fv/Fm, and lower PSII core proteins, indicating photo-oxidative damage. 5. Complementation confirmed OsCRTISO identity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: wild-type transcript detected by RT-PCR; phs3-1 transcript slightly shorter due to altered splicing
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light dependence is important because CRTISO activity can be partially substituted by photoisomerization; dark-grown seedlings accumulated prolycopene
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: photo-oxidative damage; faster water loss; abnormal chloroplast development; reduced photosystem II performance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Non-lethal variegated phenotype likely reflects partial compensation by light-driven photoisomerization.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: rice actin1 promoter (constitutive) for β-OsLCY RNAi construct
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-cyclic carotenoid products leading to β-carotene/xanthophylls
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes lycopene to form β-ionone ring-containing carotenoids in the β-branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes seed dormancy and prevents pre-harvest sprouting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss or knockdown of β-OsLCY causes lycopene accumulation, reduced ABA, increased GA in seeds, photo-oxidative damage, and pre-harvest sprouting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant sequencing; RT-PCR reduction; HPLC metabolite profiling; RNAi knockdown; ABA/GA measurement; ROS staining; chlorophyll fluorescence; western blot of PSII proteins; complementation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phs4-1 and phs4-2 carried frameshifting deletions in β-OsLCY and had strongly reduced transcript levels. 2. Mutants accumulated lycopene, including pink embryos, indicating blockage at lycopene cyclization. 3. ABA content decreased in mutants, whereas GA increased in phs4 seeds, reducing the ABA/GA ratio associated with PHS. 4. β-OsLCY RNAi plants phenocopied photobleaching, ROS accumulation, reduced NPQ and Fv/Fm, and loss of PSII core proteins. 5. Genomic complementation rescued the mutant phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transcript barely detectable in phs4 alleles compared with wild type
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light conditions exposed photobleaching/photo-oxidation; high humidity field conditions enabled PHS screening
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: albino or photobleached phenotype; lethal homozygous seedlings in mutant alleles; oxidative stress; impaired photosystem II; altered ABA/GA balance
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: putative lycopene ε-cyclase/β-cyclase complex (inferred in discussion)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes rice endosperm lacks PSY expression, explaining why lycopene accumulates in the embryo but not endosperm; β-OsLCY RNAi was driven by the rice actin1 promoter.
